author: mvafin
title: JS API E2E Electron cleanup fails on Windows - Permission denied removing locked electron.exe/dll
signature: rm cannot remove electron.exe Permission denied|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: JS API (24)
failed_jobs.symptom: E2E after-all cleanup hook fails: rm -rf Permission denied on electron.exe/dll

key_errors: exec error: Error: Command failed: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project
1) "after all" hook for "should run electron package and verify output": Error: Command failed: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project
root_cause: The E2E test for the Electron app ran successfully (2 tests passing), but the 'after all' cleanup hook uses 'rm -rf' (via bash on Windows) to remove the demo-electron-app-project directory. On Windows runners, electron.exe and its DLLs remain locked by the OS after the Electron process exits, causing 'Permission denied' errors. This is a transient Windows file-locking issue unrelated to the PR changes which touch only the PyTorch frontend.
reproduction_steps: Run the JS API E2E test on a Windows runner: cd src/bindings/js/node && npm run test:e2e
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed job - the cleanup failure is transient and unrelated to the PR.
Long-term fix: replace 'rm -rf' in the E2E after hook with a Windows-compatible cleanup that waits for file handles to be released (e.g., using rimraf or a retry loop).
Consider adding error handling in the 'after all' hook so cleanup failures do not cause test suite failures.
prevention_strategies: Use a Node.js-native directory removal (rimraf / fs.rmSync with retries) instead of shell 'rm -rf' in the E2E test cleanup hook to handle Windows file-locking gracefully.
historical_context: This is a known recurring issue: pattern 85df26485ff568d8 has been seen 6 times since 2026-07-09. Previous occurrences resolved with a simple re-run.
